metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the LLM provider call failed: Streamed response ended without content or tool calls

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'CI prompt/routing drift-parity stays green' with a concrete proving command (e.g., `pytest tests/prompt_routing/ -q` or equivalent) and define what 'drift-parity' means (exact test suite / passing threshold).
findings.finding: The AC 'CI prompt/routing drift-parity stays green' names no CI system, no test command, and no definition of 'drift-parity', so no agent can produce evidence of it passing without stopping to ask what to run.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — first bullet: 'CI prompt/routing drift-parity stays green'
findings.suggested_fix: Name the specific CI job or local command (e.g., `make test-prompt-routing`) and state the pass condition (e.g., 'all N routing-consistency tests pass with exit 0').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for the Pass-2/4 confinement claim (e.g., `git diff HEAD -- pass2/ pass4/` shows no changes, or a named CI assertion on those paths).
findings.finding: AC2 ('T3 four-class scale confined to Pass-1 + sidecar; Pass 2/4 unchanged') has no proving command — there is no grep, diff, or structural check specified to confirm Pass-2 and Pass-4 rubric files were not modified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — second bullet: 'T3 four-class scale confined to T3 Pass-1 + sidecar; Pass 2/4 unchanged'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC2: 'Verified by: `git diff origin/main -- <pass2-path> <pass4-path>` returns empty; or CI path-filter gate on those directories stays green.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite this AC to state a specific observable outcome (e.g., 'all routing-consistency tests in <suite> pass without new failures introduced by prompt edits') instead of the undefined term 'drift-parity'.
findings.finding: 'CI prompt/routing drift-parity stays green' uses the subjective/undefined term 'drift-parity', which is not a specific observable outcome and cannot be evaluated deterministically in-session.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Convert AC3 from an acceptance criterion into an explicit out-of-scope / decision-record note (e.g., move it to a 'Non-goals' or 'Rejected scope' section) so the AC list contains only falsifiable, deliverable-mapped criteria.
findings.finding: AC3 ('REJECTED here: T9 RETIRE-row rule (nit)') is an acceptance criterion with no corresponding deliverable in the What section — it verifies an absence/rejection rather than a shipped artifact, creating an orphan AC that cannot be meaningfully verified against produced work.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Non-goals / Rejected scope' section to the plan body: 'T9 RETIRE-row rule: considered and rejected (nit; see discussion).' Remove AC3 from the Acceptance Criteria list entirely.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete validation mechanism to the ACs — e.g., a before/after comparison on a known-FP or known-miss test case, or a named example finding that the enrichment would correctly catch/suppress where the old rubric failed.
findings.finding: The plan's acceptance criteria contain no concrete validation mechanism demonstrating that findings actually get sharper — only a no-regression CI check ('drift-parity stays green') and a completion check ('each criterion rubric updated as above'). There is no before/after comparison of finding quality, no FP-rate target, and no dogfooding scenario that would show the enrichments produce the promised improvement.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'Run the updated criteria against [N] archived plan-review test cases; demonstrate that at least one previously missed finding is now surfaced (or one previous FP is now suppressed) for each enriched criterion.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the parenthetical labels 'G-8' and 'T3-LOCAL' in the T3 bullet: either link them to a real ticket/ADR/doc in the repo, or remove them if they are stale internal shorthand.
findings.finding: The plan uses parenthetical identifiers 'G-8' and 'T3-LOCAL' (in the T3 bullet of the What section) that have no match anywhere in the codebase, docs, or experiments directory. These labels are presented as referencing existing concepts or tracking items, but are unresolvable. An implementor editing the T3 rubric cannot determine what design scope 'G-8' or 'T3-LOCAL' is meant to anchor to.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the parenthetical label 'G-11' in the G3 bullet: link to a real ticket/ADR/experiment doc if it exists, or remove the parenthetical if it is an internal label with no current repo anchor.
findings.finding: The plan uses parenthetical identifier 'G-11' (in the G3 bullet of the What section) as a label for the existing G3 coverage mechanism. No match for 'G-11' exists anywhere in the codebase, docs, experiments, or criteria files. This is a confident assertion ('G3 (G-11): three-part coverage standard') that implies a named prior artifact, but none is findable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the parenthetical 'FP-3a' in the T5c/T10 bullet: either link it to the calibration doc (docs/calibration/T5c_trust_boundary.md) which describes the same FP class, or remove the opaque label.
findings.finding: The plan uses 'FP-3a' as a parenthetical anchor for the T5c/T10 access-framing change (What section). No match for 'FP-3a' exists anywhere in the codebase, docs, or experiments. The label is presented as identifying an existing false-positive classification or prior art, but it is unresolvable.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(FP-3a)' with a direct reference to the calibration doc: 'T5c/T10 (see docs/calibration/T5c_trust_boundary.md — the in-scope FP class)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and name the actual Pass-2 component that will 'automatically read richer T3 evidence' without changes, or remove the parenthetical justification and state the ZERO-Pass-2-change constraint as a deliberate design decision rather than as a consequence of an existing mechanism.
findings.finding: The plan states that for T3, 'the generic likelihood grader reads richer evidence' in Pass-2, implying a named 'generic likelihood grader' component exists that will automatically benefit from the richer T3 Pass-1 evidence without changes. No such component can be found under that name anywhere in the codebase — neither 'likelihood grader' nor close variants appear in src/.
findings.location: What section, T3 bullet: 'ZERO Pass-2 change (generic likelihood grader reads richer evidence)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace or define 'G-8' and 'T3-LOCAL' with resolvable references (e.g., the specific T3 checklist key names from criteria_routing.json such as 'surface_exists' or 'technical_feasibility', or a doc section path) so reviewers can locate the edit targets.
findings.finding: The plan references 'G-8' and 'T3-LOCAL' as sub-identifiers for the T3 change ("T3 (G-8, T3-LOCAL)"), but neither string exists anywhere in the repository — not as a rubric ID, doc section, file name, or symbol. They appear to be internal tracking labels the plan author uses but that have no codebase anchor.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(G-8, T3-LOCAL)' with the actual checklist keys being enriched, e.g. '(surface_exists, auth_preconditions checklist items in criteria_routing.json + plan_review_T3.md)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'G-11' with a resolvable reference (e.g., the 'coverage_map' checklist key in criteria_routing.json, or the relevant doc section) so the G3 edit target is locatable by reviewers.
findings.finding: The plan references 'G-11' as a sub-identifier for the G3 change ("G3 (G-11)"), but this string does not exist anywhere in the repository as a rubric ID, document section, file, or symbol.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'FP-3a' with a resolvable reference (e.g., a docs/experiments file path + section, or the specific T5c checklist key 'access_classification') so the motivating FP analysis can be located.
findings.finding: The plan references 'FP-3a' as a sub-identifier for the T5c/T10 change ("T5c/T10 (FP-3a)"), but this string does not exist anywhere in the repository.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'recorded in Pass-1 evidence[] + sidecar' means (a) the LLM prompt instructs the model to format its evidence[] output using the four-class labels (prompt-only, no schema change) or (b) a new structured field is added to the sidecar payload / Pass-1 finding schema (code change required); if (b), enumerate sidecar.py and passes.py as edit targets.
findings.finding: The plan states this is 'pure prompt work' / 'prompt-only', but the T3 bullet says 'four-class evidence classification (Verified/Partial/Unverified/Contradicted) recorded in Pass-1 evidence[] + sidecar'. Writing new evidence-class fields to the sidecar (sidecar.py build_payload) is a behavioral change beyond prompt enrichment — sidecar.py currently stores criterion IDs, severities, and finding prose, not a T3-specific evidence classification enum. This is ambiguous scope.
findings.location: What section — T3 bullet: 'four-class evidence classification … recorded in Pass-1 evidence[] + sidecar'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py (and any eval dataset files it loads) as consumers of the E6, T3, and G3 rubric changes, and confirm the proposed enrichments are compatible with existing eval recall/anti-FP cases or flag which cases need updating.
findings.finding: The eval recall tests in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py pin specific E6 behavioral assertions (e.g., the R-E6-cutover-no-live-ac recall case) that are consumers of the E6 rubric content. The plan does not enumerate these tests as affected consumers that may need updating if the E6 defect taxonomy changes.
findings.location: What section — E6 bullet; AC line 1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale for encoding T3's four-class scale (Verified/Partial/Unverified/Contradicted) in free-text `evidence[]` rather than a typed field — e.g., state that free-text is chosen to avoid a Pass-2 contract change and that the classification is recoverable from sidecar prose for v1 calibration purposes.
findings.finding: The plan states a constraint ('ZERO Pass-2 change') for the T3 four-class evidence encoding design but gives no positive rationale for WHY encoding the Verified/Partial/Unverified/Contradicted classification in free-text evidence[] is preferred over a typed field — a non-trivial design choice because free-text evidence[] is human-readable but not machine-queryable, affecting whether the four-class signal is recoverable for offline calibration from the sidecar.
findings.suggested_fix: In the T3 bullet, add: 'Free-text encoding in evidence[] chosen deliberately to avoid a Pass-2 contract change; the four-class label is readable from sidecar prose for offline calibration in v1 — a typed evidence_class field can be added in a future WS if machine-queryable classification is needed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the value semantics of each four-class label (Verified/Partial/Unverified/Contradicted) in the T3 rubric enrichment, specify which output field carries it (a dedicated `evidence_class` string or a structured sub-field), and reconcile the 'recorded in sidecar' claim against sidecar.py's explicit exclusion of `evidence[]`.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a four-class evidence scale (Verified/Partial/Unverified/Contradicted) for T3 but never defines the value semantics of each class, leaves the structural encoding path ambiguous (P1Finding.evidence is list[str]; sidecar.py:236 explicitly drops evidence[] from the sidecar payload), and does not specify which field carries the class label.
findings.location: What section — T3 (G-8, T3-LOCAL) bullet; sidecar.py build_payload (evidence excluded at line 236)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a discrete `evidence_class: str` field to P1Finding (or a T3-specific sub-field), define the four closed values and their decision criteria in the T3 prompt, and either add `evidence_class` to sidecar.py's `_slim()` or restate the plan as 'Pass-1 evidence[] text only, not separately stored in sidecar'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve 'T3-LOCAL': either remove it (if T3 already covers the local-CLI case), or define it as a distinct routing entry + prompt file with its own id, exec tier, and checklist.
findings.finding: 'T3-LOCAL' is referenced in the plan as a rubric alias (parenthetical 'G-8, T3-LOCAL') but does not exist in criteria_routing.json or any reviewer file — it is an undefined-but-referenced entity.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate all nine verify-command defect types (not just examples) in the plan or directly in the updated E6 rubric prompt so a prompt author can implement the complete taxonomy.
findings.finding: The E6 enrichment names 'nine ways a present command lies' as a defect taxonomy but enumerates only 4-5 examples; the complete nine-item set is never listed anywhere in the plan, leaving the taxonomy incomplete for a prompt author to implement.
findings.location: What section — E6 bullet; plan_review_E6.md (existing prompt)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the three annotation types (bypass-annotation, scope-narrowing, partial-without-remainder) and the three-part coverage standard sub-checks in the updated G3 rubric prompt, co-located with the existing 4-bucket vocabulary, with a decision rule for when each annotation applies.
findings.finding: The G3 enrichment introduces three named annotation types ('name bypass-annotation / scope-narrowing / partial-without-remainder') as part of a 'three-part coverage standard', but these terms are not defined anywhere in the plan or in the existing plan_review_G3.md, leaving the decision protocol vocabulary undefined.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fail-safe default class for the T3 evidence probe when the external surface is unreachable or unprobeable (following the passes.py precedent of explicit DEFAULT constants), and state this in the T3 rubric enrichment.
findings.finding: The T3 four-class scale has no defined fallback for when the agentic finder cannot probe the external surface (e.g. --help unavailable, endpoint unreachable, auth required to inspect): which class applies — 'Unverified' or 'Partial' — is unspecified, yet the distinction directly affects Pass-2 grading ('ZERO Pass-2 change (generic likelihood grader reads richer evidence)').
findings.suggested_fix: Designate 'Unverified' as the explicit fail-safe default (analogous to _COMPLETION_CONTAINMENT_DEFAULT) and state it in the T3 rubric instruction.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
